fields.summary: Update YARN daemon startup/shutdown scripts to include Router service
Enable YARN RM scale out via federation using multiple RM's

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

fields.description: YARN-5410 introduce a new YARN service, i.e. Router. This jira proposes to update YARN daemon startup/shutdown scripts to include Router service.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

This message was automatically generated.

[~giovanni.fumarola], the patch looks generally ok, but since we are introducing the router in this patch, you should probably remove all the backward compatibility sections.
+1 pending.
Thanks [~asuresh] for the review. The new patch addresses your comment.

{code}
+# Specify the max heapsize for the Router.  If no units are
+# given, it will be assumed to be in MB.
+# This value will be overridden by an Xmx setting specified in either
+# HADOOP_OPTS and/or YARN_ROUTER_OPTS.
+# Default is the same as HADOOP_HEAPSIZE_MAX.
+#export YARN_ROUTER_HEAPSIZE=
+
{code}

The \_HEAPSIZE vars are only used for backward compatibility.  All new daemons in 3.x use _OPTS to set heap.
Thanks [~aw]. I updated the patch with your comment (V4).

This message was automatically generated.

+1
Will commit this shortly. Thanks for the review [~aw]

[~giovanni.fumarola], Was just trying to apply the patch, looks like there are some line ending issues.
{code}
$ patch -p1 -i YARN-6691-YARN-2915.v4.patch --dry-run
checking file hadoop-yarn/bin/yarn
checking file hadoop-yarn/bin/yarn.cmd
Hunk #1 FAILED at 130 (different line endings).
Hunk #2 FAILED at 151 (different line endings).
Hunk #3 FAILED at 248 (different line endings).
Hunk #4 FAILED at 317 (different line endings).
4 out of 4 hunks FAILED
checking file hadoop-yarn/conf/yarn-env.sh
{code}
Also, can you please recreate the patch against hadoop root dir ?

You can't use /usr/bin/patch on a git binary patch file. smart-apply-patch does work, however, which is why Apache Yetus didn't have a problem with it:

{code}
MacBook-Pro-4:hadoop aw$ dev-support/bin/smart-apply-patch YARN-6691
Processing: YARN-6691
YARN-6691 patch is being downloaded at Mon Jun 12 19:32:43 PDT 2017 from
  https://issues.apache.org/jira/secure/attachment/12872113/YARN-6691-YARN-2915.v4.patch -> Downloaded
Applying the patch:
Mon Jun 12 19:32:44 PDT 2017
cd /Users/aw/shared-vmware/hadoop
git apply --binary -v --stat --apply -p0 /tmp/yetus-20960.31585/patch
Applied patch hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/bin/yarn cleanly.
Applied patch hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/bin/yarn.cmd cleanly.
Applied patch hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/conf/yarn-env.sh cleanly.
 hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/bin/yarn         |    5 +++++
 hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/bin/yarn.cmd     |   13 ++++++++++++-
 hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/conf/yarn-env.sh |   12 ++++++++++++
 3 files changed, 29 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)
{code}

Thanks for the pointer [~aw].
Committed this to branch YARN-2915. Thanks [~giovanni.fumarola]
[~giovanni.fumarola], can you kindly provide a patch for branch-2.
I just uploaded the new patch for branch-2.

Also, can you please recreate the patch against hadoop root dir ?

You can't use /usr/bin/patch on a git binary patch file. smart-apply-patch does work, however, which is why Apache Yetus didn't have a problem with it:

{code}
MacBook-Pro-4:hadoop aw$ dev-support/bin/smart-apply-patch YARN-6691
Processing: YARN-6691
YARN-6691 patch is being downloaded at Mon Jun 12 19:32:43 PDT 2017 from

Mon Jun 12 19:32:44 PDT 2017
